title: wm-state: capture + restore floating windows
description: P0. build-workspace-layout walks only `nodes`, skips `floating_nodes`. Floating windows silently dropped on save. Must capture them as state entries with `floating: true` flag; restore applies `[id=X] floating enable` after placement. Verify: craft yaml with floating entry, restore recreates as floating.
design:
## Goal
Capture floating windows in saved wm-state YAML and restore them as floating on reapply. Today `build-workspace-layout` (nushell/actions/wm-state:139) walks only `ws.nodes` so any window in `ws.floating_nodes` is silently dropped from `state` and `layout`. After restore the window either disappears (never matched) or is re-tiled (loses position).

## Effort Estimate
6 hours — schema bump + save walker + restore branch + 5 tests + backward-compat.

## File map
- nushell/actions/wm-state — only file changed
  - `build-workspace-layout` (line 139) — extend to also walk `floating_nodes`
  - `get-wm-workspaces` (line 181) — add returned `floating` field
  - `list-workspaces` (line 318) — propagate `floating` into list output
  - `save` command path — serialise `floating` into YAML
  - `restore` command path (line 680 flat + line 723 rebuild) — re-float entries after placement
- ~/.cache/wm-state.yaml — schema migrates additively (new top-level `floating:` per workspace; absent on old files = empty list)

## Schema change (additive, backward compatible)

Existing YAML keeps `name`, `layout`, `state`. New optional sibling per workspace:

```yaml
- name: acag
  layout: { splitv: { tabbed: { '1': kitty, '2': firefox } } }
  state:
    '1': { wid: 12582925, pid: 18383, class: kitty }
    '2': { wid: 14680067, pid: 5890, class: firefox }
  floating:                # NEW; absent on old files = []
    - id: f1               # stable per save, used as match key
      class: pavucontrol
      wid: 56623113
      pid: 22841
      session_type: thin    # optional, same enrichment as state[]
      session: ...
      geometry: { x: 1000, y: 600, width: 600, height: 400 }
```

Reasoning:
- Floating windows have no container path → cannot key into `layout` body. Separate list mirrors the i3 tree shape (ws.floating_nodes is a sibling of ws.nodes).
- IDs `f1, f2, …` namespaced under `f` so they cannot collide with tiled `1, 2, …` ids used for marks during rebuild.
- `geometry` always recorded (not optional) so cross-session restore has something to apply; absent geometry = floating window with whatever default i3 gives.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Add helper `collect-floating [ws]` that returns `[{id, class, wid, pid, geometry}]` from `ws.floating_nodes`. IDs are `$"f(idx + 1)"`.
- [ ] In `build-workspace-layout`, do NOT inline floats into `layout` body. Return new shape: `{ layout, state, floating }` (extra field added to record).
- [ ] In `get-wm-workspaces` (line 181) propagate `floating` into per-ws record.
- [ ] In `list-workspaces` (line 318) merge `floating` into output and run the same enrichment (`enrich-state-entry`) so session_type/session is captured.
- [ ] In save serialiser: write `floating: []` only when non-empty (keep diffs small for tiled-only workspaces).
- [ ] In match phase: extend match-wids to walk `floating[]` alongside `state{}`. Match key for floating = (class, optional session). On match, restore re-floats. On miss, spawn via existing `spawn-for` then re-float.
- [ ] In flat restore (line 683): after the per-id loop emits `floating disable, move ...`, emit a second pass for floating entries: `[id=$wid] floating enable, move position $x $y, resize set $w $h`.
- [ ] In rebuild restore (line 723): re-floating happens AFTER append_layout + mark moves, on the same workspace. Order: park → append_layout → move-by-mark (tiled only) → re-float (floating only) → kill-anchors.
- [ ] Read path: `from yaml` will return `null` for missing `floating:` → coerce to `[]` everywhere with `default []`. Old YAML files keep working.
- [ ] Verify scratchpad path: `find-workspaces` already filters `__i3_scratch` (line 167). Double-check floating in scratchpad does NOT leak into another workspace's `floating[]`.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] **save**: workspace with one tiled kitty + one floating pavucontrol writes both — `state` has tiled, `floating[]` has the float. Verified by `wm-state save --output yaml | grep -A2 floating:`.
- [ ] **restore happy path** (same session): floating wid stays alive across save → modify layout → restore. After restore: `swaymsg -t get_tree | jq` shows the wid in `floating_nodes` of target ws with geometry within 5px of saved.
- [ ] **restore cross-session** (live wid is dead, class+session match in tree): float wid is matched and re-floated via `[id=$new_wid] floating enable`. Test: kill+respawn pavucontrol between save and restore.
- [ ] **restore unmatched floating** (no live match): existing `spawn-for` runs + post-spawn `floating enable + move position`. Polled via `get_tree` until window appears (no fixed sleep — uses bd-2ko.3 polling once it lands; for now, bounded `poll-for-window class timeout=2s` helper local to this task).
- [ ] **backward compat**: old YAML without `floating:` field still loads. Test: load `tests/fixtures/wm-state-v1.yaml` (snapshot from before this change), assert no error, assert restore reaches "already matches, skip" or runs flat path without raising.
- [ ] **floating-only workspace**: ws with `state: {}` and `floating: [{...}]` saves and restores without "no state entries" filter dropping it. Update `where (($r.state | columns) | length) > 0` (line 188) → `where (($r.state | columns | length) + ($r.floating | length)) > 0`.
- [ ] **dry-run no side effects**: `restore --dry-run` prints `floating enable` ipc command without sending it. No `tmux-start` runs for floating spawn. (Couples to bd-2ko.7.)
- [ ] All five tests in `tests/wm-state-floating.test.nu` pass; `nu tests/wm-state-floating.test.nu` exits 0.

## Tests (each names a real bug it catches)

```nu
# tests/wm-state-floating.test.nu

# catches: build-workspace-layout silently drops floating_nodes (current bug)
test_save_captures_floating_window
  craft fake tree with ws.floating_nodes=[{window: 99, name: x, class: pavucontrol}]
  expect: result.floating == [{id: 'f1', class: 'pavucontrol', wid: 99, ...}]

# catches: restore re-tiles floating window because no `floating enable` issued
test_restore_reapplies_floating_state
  load yaml with one tiled + one float
  drive restore against mock ipc
  expect: ipc-raw called with regex '\[id=\d+\] floating enable.*'

# catches: empty `state` filter drops ws when only floating windows present
test_floating_only_workspace_not_filtered_out
  craft tree: ws with state={} and floating=[{...one entry}]
  expect: get-wm-workspaces returns this ws (current code drops it via line 188)

# catches: missing field crash when reading older YAML
test_backward_compat_yaml_without_floating_field
  feed yaml without `floating:` key
  expect: load succeeds, normalises to floating=[], no error message

# catches: same wid double-handled (would tile then float race)
test_no_double_apply_when_wid_in_state_and_floating
  if same wid appears in both state{} and floating[] (data error), restore must
  apply floating last and not race; assert order in emitted commands.
```

## Key Considerations (SRE review)

**Schema migration safety**
- Additive only. Old YAML files (`floating:` absent) MUST keep loading. Test `test_backward_compat_yaml_without_floating_field` is the gate.
- Reverse direction (downgrade): a yaml with `floating: [...]` loaded by old wm-state binary will silently drop the float on next save — acceptable (lossy, not corrupt).

**Geometry semantics**
- i3/sway `rect.{x, y, width, height}` are screen-relative for floating windows. We persist as-is. On restore, `move position $x $y` is screen-relative too — correct.
- Edge case: monitor layout differs between save and restore (laptop docked vs undocked). Floating window may appear off-screen. Mitigation: clamp via `floating disable; floating enable` if `move position` IPC errors — for THIS task we defer clamping (out of scope) and document it as a known limitation in spec.
- Out of scope: dpi scaling. Sway scales geometry; assume same scale at restore time.

**Cross-session match**
- Existing `pick-live-match` (line 284) keys on wid → pid → session → class. Reuse unchanged for floating list — same match key, same fallback chain.
- After cross-session restore the new wid is fresh. `[id=$new_wid] floating enable` works because we already resolved `$new_wid` from the live tree.

**Unmatched / spawn path**
- `spawn-for` returns shell command. After exec we MUST poll the new window before issuing `floating enable`. Polling helper for this task only — replace once bd-2ko.3 lands an event-based primitive.
- Risk: spawn-for's `tmux-start` side effect on dry-run (bd-2ko.7). Floating restore amplifies this — gate behind `if not $dry_run`.

**Sticky / scratchpad**
- `find-workspaces` (line 167) filters `__i3_scratch`. Verify a sticky window is NOT duplicated across workspaces' `floating[]`. Sticky lives in `ws.floating_nodes` of CURRENT ws — collected once, not multiple times.
- Test fixture should include a sticky window scenario; if behaviour wrong, fix in this task.

**Concurrency / IPC ordering**
- Sleeps between `move container` and `floating enable` ARE necessary today (i3 timing). Use 100ms like neighbours. Document as pending dependency on bd-2ko.3 (event-based sync).
- Floating enable + move position issued as a single compound ipc command (`[id=$wid] floating enable, move position $x $y, resize set $w $h`) so i3 atomises them.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No `try { … } catch { }` around ipc-raw calls — failure to enable floating is a real error, must propagate so `--rebuild` rollback (bd-2ko.2) can catch it.
- ❌ No silent default for missing `floating` field at SAVE time — only at READ time. If save sees `floating_nodes` it MUST emit `floating: [...]`.
- ❌ No fixed `sleep 500ms` for "let the spawn settle". Use bounded poll on `get_tree`.
- ❌ No production helper that swallows ipc exit codes; if `floating enable` fails surface the error.

## Verification
```bash
# Unit tests
nu tests/wm-state-floating.test.nu

# Live smoke (manual)
cp ~/.cache/sway-state.yaml /tmp/wm-state-backup.yaml
swaymsg '[class="pavucontrol"] floating enable, move position 100 100, resize set 600 400'
nu nushell/actions/wm-state save
grep -A4 'floating:' ~/.cache/sway-state.yaml         # expect entry present
swaymsg '[class="pavucontrol"] kill'
sleep 1
pavucontrol &                                          # respawn — fresh wid
sleep 1
nu nushell/actions/wm-state restore                    # cross-session path
swaymsg -t get_tree | jq '.. | objects | select(.type=="floating_con") | .name'
cp /tmp/wm-state-backup.yaml ~/.cache/sway-state.yaml  # restore real state
```

## Out of scope (file follow-ups, NOT in this task)
- Geometry clamping for monitor layout changes between save and restore.
- DPI / scale handling.
- Replacing the per-task poll helper with bd-2ko.3 event-based sync (separate task).
- Dry-run side-effect cleanup for spawn-for `tmux-start` — covered by bd-2ko.7.

title: wiki-oxide MCP: concurrent calls timeout (shared probe URI + race in _request)
description:
Parallel wiki_preview/wiki_references calls timeout. Root causes in claude/mcp/wiki-oxide/server.py:

1. All synthetic-probe tools (wiki_preview, wiki_references) use the same URI .wiki-mcp-probe.md with version 1. Parallel didOpen on same URI confuses markdown-oxide.
2. LspClient._request uses a single response_event for all in-flight requests; concurrent waiters clear each other's set events causing timeouts/starvation.
3. next_id increment is not under self.lock.

Repro: call wiki_preview 3x in parallel. First succeeds, others time out after 8s.

Fix: per-request threading.Event (or queue.Queue keyed by rid), unique probe URI per call (e.g. include rid or uuid), guard next_id with lock.
notes:
Fixed in claude/mcp/wiki-oxide/server.py (commit 006d96e):

Two-layer root cause:
1. LspClient._request races: single response_event for all in-flight requests + unlocked next_id. Fixed via per-rid threading.Event + id_lock (kept for wiki_search/wiki_tags parallelism).
2. markdown-oxide hover wedges after ~3 synthetic-doc opens regardless of URI scheme/location (verified upstream via direct LSP probe; first 3 OK then timeout). No serialization fix possible.

Workaround: bypass LSP hover/references entirely.
- wiki_preview now reads file + rg backlinks (no LSP).
- wiki_references now pure rg (matches [[name]], [[name#h]], [[name|alias]], ](name.md)).
- Dropped dead LspClient.hover_at and references_at.

Verified 5 concurrent preview + 7 ref calls all <60ms, zero timeouts.
Requires Claude Code MCP reconnect to load new code.
close_reason: Verified live: 3 parallel wiki_preview + 1 wiki_references all return correctly. Hover bypass works, no timeouts.

title: wm-state: rebuild loses sibling order with mixed leaf+container at same level
description:
When a container body has mixed leaf and container siblings (e.g. splitv{1, splith{2,3}, 4}), build-container-plan creates ONE anchor per container. Both leaves 1 and 4 map to the same anchor wm-anchor-root.

After append_layout the anchor sits in position 0. Both leaves move-to-mark next to it. After kill-anchor the original order [leaf1, splith, leaf4] is not preserved — leaves end up adjacent to each other with splith on one side.

Fix options:
- Create a separate anchor for each leaf RUN between containers (not one per container)
- Or insert empty 'gap' cons to mark sibling positions and move leaves to those individually
- Or reject as known limitation: 'i3 layouts mixing leaves and containers at same level not preserved on --rebuild'

Edge case discovered during user testing of bd-2ko epic. Existing test fixtures (nested-siblings, workspace-layout) use either all-leaves OR all-containers per level — mixed case not covered.

title: wm-state: pure-logic stubs missed runtime nu parser error
description:
bd-2ko.2 rollback used mut+closure pattern. Pure-logic test stubs never imported the actual file, so the runtime parse error (mut variable capture in closure) escaped 14 reviewer audits. User caught it on real invocation.

Add regression: a meta-test that runs nu against wm-state itself to catch parse errors. Or change test pattern to import the production file via 'use' rather than reimplementing logic in stubs.
